common.no_examples_found: No examples found matching your search.
common.no_apps_found: No applications found matching your search.

interactive.title: Interactive Examples
interactive.description: Explore hands-on tutorials, demonstrations, and interactive examples of Reticulum-Go in action.

apps.title: Applications
apps.description: Discover applications and tools built with Reticulum-Go, including Ren Browser and Micron Parser Go.

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: Browser tools powered by Reticulum-Go and related WASM modules. Cached by the site PWA for offline use after first visit.

micron_editor.title: Micron Editor
micron_editor.description: Live Micron markup editor with WASM preview via Micron-Parser-Go.
micron_editor.page_blurb: Write Micron and see a live HTML preview. Runs entirely in your browser with Micron-Parser-Go WASM.
micron_editor.no_colors: No F/B colors in this page

reticulum_guide.title: Reticulum Guide
reticulum_guide.description: Visual tutorials on destinations, identities, announces, cryptography, packets, links, messaging, and interfaces, grounded in the official Reticulum manual.
reticulum_guide.page_blurb: Interactive explainers that keep the Zen of Reticulum in mind: sovereign networks, destinations not addresses, encryption by default.
reticulum_guide.path_blurb: Work through chapters in order. Each builds on the last.
reticulum_guide.pathfinder_note: PATHFINDER_M is {m}. Hop bytes at or above this value are rejected at unpack.
reticulum_guide.alpha_warning: This guide is alpha. Steps, interactives, and wording may change and may not always be accurate. Prefer the official Reticulum manual and project docs when you need a definitive reference.

rnode_flasher.title: RNODE Flasher
rnode_flasher.description: Flash and provision RNode firmware from the browser. Official microReticulum upload and Tiny-Reticulum-Go catalog support.
rnode_flasher.page_blurb: Connect over USB, Bluetooth, IP, or Simulator then flash and optionally provision your board.
rnode_flasher.tiny_coming_soon: Tiny-Reticulum-Go builds are not published yet. The catalog slot and download path are ready for when artifacts exist.
rnode_flasher.catalog_empty: No bundled builds for this source yet.
rnode_flasher.permission_hint: Serial and Bluetooth prompts only appear after you click Connect over HTTPS.
rnode_flasher.progress_idle: Progress appears here after you start a flash.
rnode_flasher.provision_blurb: Virgin boards can receive EEPROM provision and a firmware hash after a successful flash.
rnode_flasher.done_blurb: Reconnect the board to Reticulum and verify radio traffic when ready.
rnode_flasher.offline_banner: You are offline. Using bundled catalog and any firmware cached in this browser.
rnode_flasher.sim_hint: Use Simulator to try the full flash and provision flow without hardware.
rnode_flasher.engine_note_chromium: Web Serial and Web Bluetooth work in Chromium based browsers when enabled (Chrome, Edge, Brave, Opera).
rnode_flasher.engine_note_firefox: Firefox does not implement Web Serial or Web Bluetooth. Use IP bridge or Simulator here, or flash from Chromium.
rnode_flasher.engine_note_webkit: Safari and other WebKit browsers do not implement Web Serial or Web Bluetooth. Use IP bridge or Simulator here, or flash from Chromium.
rnode_flasher.engine_note_other: Web Serial and Web Bluetooth need a Chromium based browser. IP bridge and Simulator work in this browser.
rnode_flasher.brave_blocked_blurb: This looks like Brave, and Web Serial or Web Bluetooth is not available for this page yet.
rnode_flasher.brave_enable_serial_step1: Open brave://flags and search for serial. If Web Serial API appears, set it to Enabled and restart Brave.
rnode_flasher.brave_enable_serial_step2: Open brave://settings/content/serialPorts and allow sites to ask to connect.
rnode_flasher.brave_enable_serial_step3: Lower Shields for this site if needed, then click Connect again and approve the prompt.
rnode_flasher.brave_enable_bluetooth_step1: Open brave://flags and search for bluetooth. Enable Web Bluetooth related flags if they appear, then restart Brave.
rnode_flasher.brave_enable_bluetooth_step2: Open brave://settings/content and allow Bluetooth for this site when prompted.
rnode_flasher.brave_enable_bluetooth_step3: Lower Shields for this site if needed, then click Connect again and approve the prompt.
rnode_flasher.brave_enable_reload: After changing flags or settings, reload this page. Newer Brave builds may already enable Serial by default.

bot_builder.title: Bot Builder
bot_builder.description: Visual builder for LXMF bots powered by LXMFy-Go. Design handlers, destinations, and bot flows without starting from a blank file.

donate.title: Donate
donate.subtitle: Support the development and maintenance of Reticulum-Go.
donate.intro: Choose a platform below or send Monero directly.
donate.meta_description: Support Reticulum-Go development. 50% of donations go to Mark Qvist, creator of the Reticulum reference implementation at reticulum.network.
donate.markqvist_share: 50% of donations go to Mark Qvist, creator of the Reticulum reference implementation.

source.title: Source Code
source.subtitle: Pick where you want to get Reticulum-Go.
source.intro: The official repository lives on rngit over Reticulum. GitHub and Lavaforge are public mirrors.
source.meta_description: Get Reticulum-Go source from official rngit over Reticulum, download the latest GitHub release zip from this site, or use GitHub and Lavaforge mirrors.
source.download_zip_pending: Preparing the latest zip. Refresh in a moment if the download fails.

rngit.title: rngit
rngit.description: Official source over Reticulum. Clone with an RNS-capable git client, or open the Micron page in NomadNet and Ren Browser.
rngit.page_blurb: Same project as a Micron page for NomadNet and other mesh clients.

github.title: GitHub
github.description: Public mirror used for CI and releases, plus browsers and conventional git workflows.

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: Have questions or want to get involved? Reach out to the team behind Reticulum-Go.
contact.meta_description: Contact the Reticulum-Go team via LXMF or email.
contact.quad4_body: For general inquiries about Quad4 Software or business partnerships.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: We collect zero personal data about you.
privacy.meta_description: Reticulum-Go privacy policy. We collect zero personal data. No analytics, trackers, ads, or accounts.
privacy.summary: This site and its browser tools collect ZERO personal data about you. We do not sell, share, or store profiles of visitors.

no_analytics.title: No analytics or tracking
no_analytics.body: We do not use analytics services, advertising pixels, fingerprinting scripts, or telemetry. Your visits are not scored, profiled, or reported to third parties by us.

no_accounts.title: No accounts
no_accounts.body: There is no sign-in, no user profiles, and no account database. You do not need an identity with us to use the site.

local_processing.title: Local processing
local_processing.body: WASM demos, the RNode flasher, and similar tools run in your browser. Identities, messages, and flash sessions stay on your device unless you choose to send something yourself, such as an email to contact us.

cookies.title: Preference cookies only
cookies.body: We may store small preference cookies for theme and language so the site remembers your choices. These are not used for tracking or advertising.

third_parties.title: Third-party links
third_parties.body: Links to GitHub, donation platforms, and other external sites are operated by those parties under their own policies. We do not sell or share personal data with them because we do not collect it.

home.title: Reticulum-Go
home.subtitle: The resilient, sovereign networking stack for everyone. Now implemented in Go for high performance and portability.

encrypted.title: Encrypted by Default
encrypted.description: Reticulum uses strong, modern cryptography end to end. On-wire layouts match the Python reference so peers stay compatible.

resilient.title: Resilient & Sovereign
resilient.description: No central authorities, no servers to shut down. Build your own infrastructure over any medium - radio, fiber, satellite, landlines, or the internet.

go.title: Go Implementation
go.description: Built with Go for portability, auditability, and concurrency. Run it from embedded devices to cloud servers.

interop.title: Full RNS Interoperability
interop.description: Wire-compatible with the official Python Reticulum (RNS). Packets, links, and cryptography work peer-to-peer.

sandbox.title: Secure by Design
sandbox.description: The native daemon enables an OS sandbox by default. The WASM build runs inside the browser sandbox of the host runtime.

modular.title: Modular & Embeddable
modular.description: Use it as a daemon, Go library, C ABI (librns), control API, or WASM module.

coexistence.title: Expanding Reticulum's Reach
coexistence.description: Reticulum-Go was built to work with the Python reference implementation by Mark Qvist, not to replace it. The goal is to help the network and expand where Reticulum can run, while Python RNS remains the reference that defines the protocol.

ren_promo.title: Browse the Reticulum Network
ren_promo.description: Ren Browser is a modern NomadNet-compatible client built with Reticulum-Go. Render Micron pages, manage identities, and explore the mesh from desktop or mobile.

platforms.title: Supported platforms
platforms.list: Windows · macOS / iOS · Android · Debian · Ubuntu · Fedora · openSUSE · Arch · Gentoo · NixOS · Alpine · FreeBSD · OpenBSD · NetBSD · Haiku · Raspberry Pi · PowerPC · Flatpak · Docker · Firecracker · WASM

ren_browser.title: Ren Browser
ren_browser.subtitle: A modern browser for the Reticulum Network, built with Reticulum-Go.

nomadnet.title: NomadNet / Micron
nomadnet.description: Render NomadNet pages with full Micron markup support for browsing the mesh.

identities.title: Multiple Identities
identities.description: Create, import, export, and switch between Reticulum identities with ease.

interfaces.title: Interface Control
interfaces.description: Full control over network interfaces with hot reload for live configuration changes.

documents.title: Document Reader
documents.description: Read EPUB and PDF documents alongside mesh content in one place.

editor.title: Micron Editor
editor.description: Write and preview Micron pages with a built-in editor.

extensions.title: Extension System
extensions.description: JS and WASM plugins with a permissions model to extend the browser.

platforms.title: Runs Anywhere
platforms.description: Linux, Windows, macOS, Android, plus Docker and headless server builds.

i18n.title: Localized UI
i18n.description: Interface available in English, German, Italian, and Russian.

ren_browser.mobile_description: A native-feeling experience on Android with the same identities, discovery, and browsing tools.
ren_browser.get_description: Pre-built packages for Linux, Windows, macOS, and Android, plus a headless server and Docker image.
ren_browser.platforms_app_list: Linux (AppImage, Flatpak, .deb, .rpm), Windows, macOS, Android, iOS
ren_browser.platforms_web_list: Linux (amd64, arm64, armv6), FreeBSD, OpenBSD, NetBSD, Windows
ren_browser.note: Active development. Expect rough edges until v1.0.
